note: Each post is portable Markdown with YAML frontmatter (title, subtitle, date, tags, status). The `status` field marks how grounded the work is: validated > shipped > real-result > partial > partial-poc. Drop these into any static-site generator, or render from this manifest.

posts.title: Running a 230B model on a pod that can't hold it
posts.subtitle: Zero-egress, host-distributed checkpoint sharding for MiniMax-M2.7 on TPU
posts.summary: Sharding a 460 GB MoE so each TPU host stores only ~27 GB of it, by querying the engine's own per-device sharding plan. 6,678 tok/s on v6e, and a 902x v5e cliff from one missing collective.

posts.title: A 21x bandwidth gap hiding in one dynamic_slice
posts.subtitle: Chasing the real decode throughput ceiling of a 229B MoE on TPU v5e and v6e
posts.summary: Why a decode loop ran at ~80 GB/s against an 1,800 GB/s ceiling, why scan=False beats scan=True, and why TPU throughput is quantized in batch size. Ceiling: 6,792 tok/s on v6e.

posts.title: When a model runs fine but won't serve: compile time vs. the preemption window
posts.subtitle: Gemma 4 31B decodes correctly on TPU in seconds, and never comes up as an API server on spot capacity
posts.summary: A correct model that decodes in seconds still can't be served, because a 40-minute serving compile never finishes inside a preemptible TPU's window. Compile time as a first-class deployment constraint.

posts.title: 94.6% explained variance, then -288%: training SAEs that don't quietly die
posts.subtitle: Sparse autoencoders on 2.8B tokens of TPU activations, and the one flag that decided success
posts.summary: A JAX/TPU SAE trainer (TopK/JumpReLU/Gated/Vanilla), a dictionary that hit 94.6% explained variance, a run that collapsed to -288%, and the dead-neuron-resampling cutoff that was the whole difference.

posts.title: 37,292 experiments, 597 ideas: what an autonomous research loop actually does
posts.subtitle: Probing a Tiny Recursive Model on ARC - and watching an LLM-driven research loop run out of questions
posts.summary: A real finding (task-ID sensitivity dissociated from halting and correctness; 107/200 tasks never solved) and a cautionary meta-finding (an LLM loop re-asked 597 unique questions 23.5x each on average).

posts.title: dtmpfs: a distributed RAM filesystem in Rust
posts.subtitle: Mount the same namespace on every host, shard the bytes across the cluster's RAM
posts.summary: A transient distributed tmpfs: rendezvous-hashed 1 MiB blocks across nodes' RAM, close-to-open consistency via a per-inode generation epoch, replica read-failover, and 35 black-box acceptance cases.

posts.title: The 40-byte struct: driving a TPU from pure C
posts.subtitle: Reverse-engineering the PJRT C API to run kernels on a TPU with no Python, no JAX, no TensorFlow
posts.summary: dlopen libtpu.so, find the function table at the non-obvious offset 40 (a nested version struct), and run SPMD / dev2dev / async / serialized kernels from ~200 lines of C. All 8 examples verified on v4.

posts.title: Round-tripping an AST loses your parentheses
posts.subtitle: Transpiling V8 from C++ to Rust through Clang, and the precedence bugs that live in the gap between tree and text
posts.summary: A Clang-AST C++->Rust transpiler for V8 (49 crates, 1,206 files emitted) and the lesson that AST->source must re-derive the parentheses the parser threw away - or silently change `!(x >= 1)` into `(!x) >= 1`.

posts.title: Porting an autonomous training-optimization loop to TPU
posts.subtitle: How a not-fusing-two-jit-calls decision and a bigger batch moved val_bpb from 1.136 to 1.115
posts.summary: An autonomous train-edit-measure loop on TPU v4-8. The wins: a bigger batch that deleted gradient accumulation, remat to fit it, and NOT fusing the optimizer into the step (the JIT boundary is a memory-release boundary).
